definition: To express something clearly and effectively
example: She was able to articulate the algorithm's logic in simple terms.

definition: Giving a lot of information clearly in few words
example: A concise explanation is more valuable than a long one in interviews.

definition: Clearly show the existence or truth of something by evidence
example: Let me demonstrate this with a concrete example.

definition: To develop or present a theory in further detail
example: Could you elaborate on why you chose this data structure?

definition: To use something to maximum advantage
example: We leverage the sorted property of the array to run binary search.

definition: Done according to a fixed plan or system
example: I systematically ruled out approaches that required O(n²) time.

definition: Forming a necessary base or core; of central importance
example: Recursion is a fundamental concept in computer science.

definition: In exact terms; without vagueness
example: This is precisely why we need a base case in every recursive function.

definition: As a result; therefore
example: The loop runs n times; consequently the time complexity is O(n).

definition: Strong and effective in all conditions
example: A robust solution must handle null inputs and empty arrays.

definition: Make less severe, serious, or painful
example: We use a CDN to mitigate latency issues for global users.

definition: Make an action or process easy or easier
example: Docker helps facilitate the deployment process across environments.

definition: Make an organization or system more efficient and effective
example: We streamlined the CI/CD pipeline to reduce build times.

definition: The capacity to be changed in size or scale
example: Microservices offer better scalability than monolithic architectures.

definition: Make the best or most effective use of a resource
example: We need to optimize the database queries to reduce load times.

definition: Check or prove the validity or accuracy of something
example: Always validate user input on the server side.

definition: Combine one thing with another so that they become a whole
example: We plan to integrate Stripe for payment processing.

definition: Enclose something in or as if in a capsule
example: We encapsulate the logic inside a single class to hide complexity.

definition: Employing or involving independent units that can be combined
example: A modular design allows teams to work independently.

definition: Able to withstand or recover quickly from difficult conditions
example: A resilient system gracefully handles third-party API failures.
